<think>
  Stage 1 — Feature Sheet:
  - DOC1: On Prompt-Driven Safeguarding for Large Language Models
    - References: OpenAI (2023), Touvron et al. (2023), Jiang et al. (2023), Zheng et al. (2024a), Weidinger et al. (2021), Lester et al. (2021), Zou et al. (2023), Li et al. (2023), Zeng et al. 2024, Zheng, 2023
  - DOC2: Purple Llama CYBERSECEvAL: A Secure Coding Benchmark for Language Models
    - References: Pearce et al., 2022, Siddiq and Santos, 2022, Li et al., 2023, Khoury et al., 2023, Tihanyi et al., 2023, Zhong and Wang, 2023, Yetiştiren et al., 2023, Chen et al., 2021, Hajipour et al., 2023, Siddiq et al., 2022, Sandoval et al., 2023
  - DOC3: Navigating the Safety Landscape: Measuring Risks in Finetuning Large Language Models
    - References: Qi et al. [29], Anthropic red-teaming dataset [14], OpenAI finetuning API [28], AdamW optimizer [26], Bianchi et al. [4], [7], [30], [36], [8], [12], [42], [22], [40], [2], JailbreakBench [9]

  Stage 2 — Structure Selection and Mapping:
  - The structure should be a "mapping" because we need to map each document to its references and citations.
  - Each document will be a key, and the values will be lists of references and citations.
  - Since there is no explicit mention of citations in the provided facts, the "Citation" list will be empty for all documents.
